Name: Sam
Age & grade: 14, 9th grade.

Interests & passions:
Makes hip-hop and K-pop-style beats in FL Studio — three years of self-taught
production. Knows his way around a sidechain compressor and can hum the
difference between a 808 and a sub-bass. Currently obsessed with a New
Jeans-meets-late-J-Dilla sound — quantized to a "swing" feel rather than
straight 16ths. Fluent in K-pop lore: NewJeans, IVE, Stray Kids, plus older
SHINee and BIGBANG. Writes lyrics in his Notes app, occasionally in
Korean (with a translator) which his older sister corrects.

Hobbies & media:
- FL Studio every night for at least 45 minutes; shares loops on a private
  Discord server with three friends from school.
- Listens to "Song Exploder" (the podcast) on the bus.
- Watches K-pop dance practice videos and reaction channels (DKDKTV).
- Plays Beat Saber when his mom isn't using the headset.

Learning style & teacher notes:
Low math confidence — failed the last fractions unit (final score 41%) and
since then has been quietly disengaging from any math task that involves
ratios or division. Strong verbal: when he explains a beat structure aloud,
he uses fractions naturally ("the snare hits on the 'and' of 2 and 4, so
it's a 1/8th displaced from the kick"). He doesn't realize he's already
fluent in the underlying ideas. The instructional move that works: re-skin
math problems through beats and time signatures, then *retroactively* show
him "you just did exactly what you do every night in FL Studio." Wants to
feel smart again — he is sensitive to being singled out as struggling, so
avoid public correction. He responds well to one-on-one micro-checks
("show me on paper what you'd do in FL Studio for this"). His default
when stuck is to laugh and deflect — wait through the laugh, then redirect.
